meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance Blueprint: User Onboarding to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stuck without project ideas? Learn to build high-converting user onboarding flows using Antigravity and Claude Code to impress clients and ship faster.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing abstract ideas. The most profitable freelance projects solve the 'empty state' problem. By mastering user onboarding, you provide immediate value to clients. Use Antigravity to scaffold the UI and Claude Code to iterate on the logic, transforming a simple onboarding flow into a high-converting asset.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for freelance ambition. You stare at the screen, waiting for the 'perfect' SaaS idea, while your billable hours tick toward zero. The friction isn't a lack of creativity; it’s a lack of focus on the one feature that makes or breaks every single application: the first five minutes of the user experience.

User onboarding is the ultimate freelance 'value-add.' Clients don't just want code; they want retention. By positioning yourself as an onboarding architect, you stop selling features and start selling growth. We are going to bypass the 'no project ideas' trap by building a modular, repeatable onboarding system that you can plug into any client project immediately.

steps.title: Define the Value-First Onboarding Loop
steps.description: Identify the 'Aha!' moment for a hypothetical SaaS. We focus on getting the user to their first success in under 60 seconds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a 3-step onboarding flow for a productivity SaaS that includes user preference collection, a feature tour, and a data-sync trigger.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a React component structure for a multi-step modal based on the defined schema.'

steps.title: Scaffold the State Management
steps.description: Handle onboarding progress without bloating your main application logic. We use a context-provider pattern for clean separation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lightweight React Context provider to track onboarding step completion and persist state to localStorage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the OnboardingContext.tsx using the provided schema and ensure it handles step transitions.'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Tour Components
steps.description: Design non-intrusive UI elements that guide users without overwhelming them.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive, accessible tooltip component that attaches to DOM elements using Framer Motion for smooth transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add the Tooltip component to the existing onboarding flow and ensure it handles window resizing.'

steps.title: Implement Event-Driven Analytics
steps.description: Clients need to see the data. Add hooks to track where users drop off in your onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom hook 'useOnboardingAnalytics' that sends step-completion events to a posthog-like tracking interface.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate useOnboardingAnalytics into the step transition logic.'

steps.title: Finalize and Package for Reusability
steps.description: Convert your hard work into a standalone module that you can drop into any future client project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the onboarding module into a standalone npm-ready directory structure with clear documentation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a README.md for this onboarding module explaining how to install and configure it for new projects.'

pro_tips: Always build onboarding as a decoupled feature module; clients will pay a premium for systems that are 'plug-and-play'.
Use 'optimistic UI' for onboarding steps to ensure the application feels instantaneous, even if the backend is slow.
Never force a login before the onboarding value is demonstrated; let the user experience the 'Aha!' moment first.
Tag your onboarding steps with unique IDs so you can run A/B tests on the flow order using simple URL parameters.
Include a 'Skip' button that triggers a 'Quick Start' mode; power users hate guided tours as much as new users need them.
content.next_steps: Now that you have a modular onboarding system, package it as a private repository. The next time a client asks for a feature, you can upsell this onboarding flow as a 'Retention Accelerator' package, saving you weeks of work.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn any project into a high-retention machine. Don't just code features; build systems that scale revenue for your clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
